paper_focus.title: Low-density and high-modulus steel achieved by hypereutectic TiB2 and high Al ferrite phase
paper_focus.research_objective: To develop a novel low-density steel that mitigates the severe reduction in Young's modulus and casting/processing challenges of high-Al Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys by utilizing hypereutectic TiB2 particles and δ-ferrite in a carbon-free austenitic matrix.
material_systems: Fe-Mn-Al-Ti-B alloy (low-density triplex steel, carbon-free, austenite matrix with elongated δ-ferrite bands and micron-sized TiB2 particles)
main_process_variables: Synchrotron X-ray diffraction for dislocation density measurement
Finite element simulations of deformation and damage
